rows.source_ref: jamesagnew, 'trade fractals, this method is a winner', ForexFactory thread 1348734 (forum discussion, 41 posts), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1348734/trade-fractals-this-method-is-a-winner
rows.concept: Breakout 5 pips past a confirmed Bill Williams fractal - buy above a down-fractal swing low, sell below an up-fractal swing high - with a fixed 2:1 reward:risk.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic fractal-breakout, fixed SL 50 / TP 100 pips, H4 FX (USDCAD) data via .DWX, FTMO/DXZ-compatible; forum author admits not trading/testing and it is a generic breakout, hence low priority.
rows.notes: Base rule: down fractal -> buy stop at signal-candle high +5 pips; up fractal -> sell stop at low -5 pips; TP 100 / SL 50 pips; H4 USDCAD, 'works on any pair'. Bill Williams fractal only confirms after 2 closed bars each side, so entry must arm on the confirmed fractal to avoid the repaint that FerruFx flags. Optional confirmation-candle filter variant (page 6): take buy only if the candle right of a down fractal is higher-high+higher-low, sell only if lower-high+lower-low - fold as a parameter, not a separate row. Thread widely panned as untested spam (author has ~58 similar threads). Secondary idea by perth1985 (Heikin-Ashi 1:1 RR with MA/trend confirmation from an external video) is too VAGUE to classify - not a candidate.
